body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.

**Is neuroimaging ready for the classroom? Asystematic review of hyperscanning studies in learning.**. Neuroimage.. PMID: 37689175. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2023.120367. Whether education research can be informed by findings from neuroscience studies has been hotly debated since Bruer'sfamous claim that neuroscience and education are "abridge too far". However, this claim came before recent advancements in portable electroencephalography (EEG) and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) technologies, and second-person neuroscience techniques that brought about significant headway in understanding instructor-learner interactions in the classroom.
